paper.title: Unified Energy Exchange Theory (UEET): Synthesis of Reality through Energy Exchange

theory.name: Unified Energy Exchange Theory (UEET)
theory.summary: Proposes a discrete tetrahedral lattice of energy nodes with phase field phi; matter and antimatter as phase-opposite overlap of a single field; asymmetry factor alpha introduces residence time in nodes, yielding rest mass E=(1-alpha)mc^2 and a master equation coupling d'Alembert and nonlinear potential to topological drive. Aims to derive EM, gravity, nuclear forces, cosmology, and constants as emergent from geometry and alpha without ad-hoc dark sectors. (Author category: unified field theory / speculative phenomenology.)

postulates.statement: Matter and antimatter are phase-opposite manifestations of one energy field; interaction is oscillatory phase overlap, not only terminal annihilation—high-frequency resonance at nodal junctions; energy transitions between localized inclusion and network flow.

Visible persistent mass requires fundamental asymmetry factor alpha; imperfect phase overlap gives finite residence time of quanta in tetrahedral energy nodes, interpreted as rest mass; alpha measures resistance to perfect vacuum recycling.
Massless quanta exchange with phase fields via nodal capture, inclusion/flickering between Psi_M and Psi_Mbar at nodal frequency, producing emergent mass as effective resistance to motion.

assumptions.statement: Spacetime or vacuum substrate is well approximated by a fixed topological tetrahedral grid of energy nodes.
assumptions.rationale: Stated as one of two Occam pillars with alpha; defines discrete geometry and Planck-scale saturation C_max.

assumptions.statement: A single asymmetry parameter alpha (and its spatial/temporal gradients) governs dynamics; other constants are emergent from alpha and network geometry.
assumptions.rationale: Author claims reduction of free parameters vs Standard Model fine-tuning.

assumptions.statement: Continuum field equations (e.g. master equation for phi, Maxwell, GR limit) are valid effective descriptions of an underlying discrete nodal dynamics.
assumptions.rationale: Monograph uses PDEs and takes smooth limits while positing Planck-scale discreteness.

claims.statement: Observable rest mass-energy scales as E = (1 - alpha) m c^2 with alpha the asymmetry factor (monograph title relation).
Phase field phi obeys master PDE: box phi + 4 lambda phi(phi^2 - v^2) = (K^dagger/(2 E_P)) nabla(D phi)(D phi) coupling nonlinear vacuum structure to gauge-covariant topological drive.
Gravity, electromagnetism, and nuclear forces are resonant modes of the same nodal network—not independent fundamental interactions.

Framework is claimed to address vacuum energy magnitude, baryon asymmetry framing, Hubble tension variability, mass hierarchy without Higgs-centric narrative, and GR singularities via nodal saturation C_max.

predictions.statement: Additional long-range Yukawa-type fifth force from lattice collective mode, range lambda_5 roughly microns to meters, coupling to mass via lattice distortion.
Triple-charm baryon ccc++ mass 4785 +/- 15 MeV, angular tension sum ~1.2 deg vs tetrahedral 109.47 deg, anomalously narrow decay width per NSL.
At Planck-scale densities, discrete lattice corrections to GR become observable (e.g. near black hole horizons).
Fine-structure constant may show small deviations from ~1/137 under extreme lattice deformation or high energy density.
Cosmological constant Lambda may exhibit weak time dependence tied to H and Planck scale via dynamic lattice fluctuations (rho_Lambda ~ 10^-122 rho_Planck context).
